Answer:
1. Capital city of Australia: Canberra

Authoritative source (one URL cited):
1. The Siting and Naming of Canberra — National Capital Authority: https://www.nca.gov.au/education/canberras-history/siting-and-naming-canberra
output.action_summary: I used the authoritative Australian government page listed in the merged search results: 'The Siting and Naming of Canberra' on the National Capital Authority website (https://www.nca.gov.au/education/canberras-history/siting-and-naming-canberra). The evidence in the merged results identifies Canberra as the capital city of Australia. No additional facts were requested, and no evidence is missing.

results.title: Australian Capital Territory (Canberra) | Study Australia
results.description: As Australia's capital, Canberra boasts some of the country’s preeminent educational and cultural institutions.

results.title: The Siting and Naming of Canberra | National Capital Authority
results.description: ‘I name the capital of Australia, Canberra – the accent is on the Can’.

results.title: Founding of Canberra | National Museum of Australia
results.description: On 12 March 1913 the Governor-General’s wife, Lady Denman, announced that Canberra was the name of Australia’s new national capital.

results.title: Canberra 1913: Naming the Federal Capital of Australia | National Film and Sound Archive of Australia
results.description: On the morning of Wednesday 12 March 1913, 500 invited guests, over 700 mounted and artillery troops and a public crowd of over 3,000 locals came to witness the formal naming of Canberra. Foundation stones were laid by Governor-General Lord Thomas Denman, Prime Minister Andrew Fisher and the Minister for Home Affairs, King O’Malley. The national anthem was played and Lady Gertrude Denman announced the chosen name for the new-born federal capital.

results.title: Home - ACT Government
results.description: Canberra Aquatic Centre Design Consultation We're planning a new Canberra Aquatic Centre in the heart of Commonwealth Park.

merged_results.title: Visit the top authoritative result and extract citation text

result.intent: Open the most authoritative matching page and extract the exact wording that identifies Canberra as Australia's capital, plus page title, full URL, and any date/metadata for a reliable citation.

results.title: Canberra 1913: Naming the Federal Capital of Australia | National Film and Sound Archive of Australia
results.description: On the morning of Wednesday 12 March 1913, 500 invited guests, over 700 mounted and artillery troops and a public crowd of over 3,000 locals came to witness the formal naming of Canberra. Foundation stones were laid by Governor-General Lord Thomas Denman, Prime Minister Andrew Fisher and the Minister for Home Affairs, King O’Malley. The national anthem was played and Lady Gertrude Denman announced the chosen name for the new-born federal capital.
